concerns.text: Task T011 (simulate_EEG.py) is marked [US1] but depends on the structural adjacency matrices produced by T010 (preprocess_dMRI.py). However, T011 is listed as [P] (parallel-safe) alongside T009 and T010. T011 cannot run in parallel with T010 as it consumes T010's output artifact. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule and mislabels the task as parallel-safe.

Task T014 (metrics.py) and T015 (avalanches.py) are marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, T015 (avalanches) requires the synthetic EEG time-series generated by T011 (simulate_EEG.py) in Phase 3. While T014 and T015 might be parallel to each other, the entire Phase 4 block is implicitly dependent on the completion of Phase 3 (specifically T011). The current ordering implies Phase 4 can start immediately after Phase 2, ignoring the data dependency on US1's simulation output.

Task T019 (stats.py) is marked [P] and listed in Phase 5. It computes correlations between structural metrics (from T014) and avalanche exponents (from T015). T019 cannot execute until T014 and T015 are complete. The [P] tag suggests it can run in parallel with other Phase 5 tasks, but it is a consumer of Phase 4 outputs. The dependency chain US1 -> US2 -> US3 is not reflected in the parallel tags or phase ordering, risking execution of T019 before its inputs exist.
